document.source_title: Encouraging progress in inclusive health policies for refugees and migrants

claims.claim_text: More than 60 countries now include refugees and migrants in their national health policies.
claims.evidence_text: new data showing more than 60 countries – two thirds of those surveyed – now include them in their national health policies and laws.

claims.claim_text: Investment in refugee and migrant health delivers far-reaching dividends.
claims.evidence_text: Investment in refugee and migrant health deliver far-reaching dividends.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The directing and coordinating authority on international health within the United Nations system.
entities.evidence_text: said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General, World Health Organization.

entities.name: International Organization for Migration
entities.description: An intergovernmental organization that provides services and advice concerning migration to governments and migrants alike.
entities.evidence_text: WHO will also continue working closely with partners, including the International Organization for Migration.

events.event: Release of WHO report on health of migrants and refugees
events.evidence_text: WHO’s new report on the health of migrants and refugee shows that inclusion benefits whole societies.

metrics.name: Percentage of countries including refugees and migrants in health policies
metrics.evidence_text: new data showing more than 60 countries – two thirds of those surveyed – now include them in their national health policies and laws.

metrics.name: Percentage of countries collecting migration-related health data
metrics.evidence_text: only 37% of responding countries routinely collect, analyze and disseminate migration-related health data.

relationships.subject: World Health Organization
relationships.evidence_text: WHO will also continue working closely with partners, including the International Organization for Migration.

citations.title: Encouraging progress in inclusive health policies for refugees and migrants

source_credibility.reason: The source is the World Health Organization, a recognized authority in global health.
